steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to "specify the parts of a Foucault pendulum." The notes list three structural components: a fixed point it dangles from, a cable (roughly 26 meters long), and a weighted ball at the end of the cable. The best choice names all three parts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Foucault pendulum at the Science Museum in Krakow, Poland, includes a cable that is roughly 26 meters long.: Mentions only one part (the cable) and focuses on a specific measurement rather than specifying all structural components. ✗
  • Choice B — With a swing path that appears to change over time, a Foucault pendulum provides evidence of Earth's rotation.: Describes the pendulum's behavior and function, not its physical parts at all. ✗
  • Choice C — Dangling from a fixed point, the Foucault pendulum at the Science Museum consists of a cable with a weighted ball at the end.: Names all three structural parts: the fixed point it hangs from, the cable, and the weighted ball. This directly accomplishes the goal of specifying parts. ✓
  • Choice D — The swing path of the Foucault pendulum at the Science Museum appears to change over time.: Describes observed behavior related to Earth's rotation, not any structural component of the pendulum. ✗
That is the correct answer.
